overview: Supports general undergraduate advising questions, academic planning referrals, and campus advising coordination when students are not sure which department to start with.
Helps students understand transfer-credit evaluation, articulation questions, and how prior coursework fits degree progress.
Supports registration, enrollment verification, transcript requests, graduation clearance, and academic record updates.

office: Center for Academic Success and Achievement
overview: Provides tutoring, study skills support, time-management coaching, and academic recovery planning.
Supports career exploration, internships, resume reviews, and graduate-school preparation.
Supports student mental health, crisis referrals, stress management, and broader wellness needs that can affect academic performance.
Helps students request accommodations, coordinate documentation, and work with instructors on accessibility needs.
Answers questions about aid packages, satisfactory academic progress requirements, scholarships, billing support, and enrollment impacts.
Coordinates referrals when academic concerns overlap with wellness, accommodations, attendance issues, or broader student-support needs.

Supports student organizations, leadership development, campus involvement, and the nearest official path for students asking how to get involved in clubs or organization life at Morgan. Public leadership contacts include Danny T. Molock, Jr. and De'Jah Herndon-Johnson.

Promotes undergraduate research across disciplines and provides the main official path for research opportunities, research internships, and faculty-mentored projects. Public contacts include Dr. Lisa Brown, Director, and Mrs. Shamara Murphy, SRC Coordinator.

The Student Research Center is a registered Morgan undergraduate student organization supported by the Office of Undergraduate Research. It helps students find interdisciplinary research community, networking, and undergraduate research engagement opportunities.
